summary: Verify inbound webhooks — HMAC signature, timestamp window, replay defense — through a contract-stable interface with pluggable, attested signature-scheme adapters.

exports: class WebhookError extends Error { code: WebhookErrorCode; status: number }

invariants: Importing the part performs no I/O and never throws; configuration is validated at call time with typed errors

A request whose HMAC signature does not verify over the exact raw payload bytes — tampered payload, tampered signature, or missing signature headers — is rejected with a typed error; signature comparison is timing-safe (crypto.timingSafeEqual)
A signed timestamp outside the tolerance window (default 300 seconds, past or future) is rejected
A delivery replayed within the tolerance window is rejected (v1: in-memory per instance — serverless limitation stated in SPEC.md)
Verification operates on the raw payload bytes as received; any re-serialization that changes a single byte fails verification
All failures surface as typed WebhookError values carrying a recommended HTTP status; secret values never appear in error messages
webhookHandler acknowledges 2xx only after every registered handler has completed; verification failures return 400 with a generic body; handler failures return 500 so the vendor redelivers
notes: v1 scope is verification and dispatch. Every adapter's scheme must carry a signed timestamp — schemes without one (GitHub's X-Hub-Signature-256) cannot satisfy invariant 3 uniformly and need a capability RFC before entering. Durable (cross-instance) replay defense arrives as an additive minor with the DB story. The conformance suite lives with the implementing part until a second provider exists, then moves here (docs/02 §3-4).
